questions.question: Which specific financial obligation forced the French government to spend an increasing percentage of its budget on interest payments alone?

options.A: The 2 billion livres debt to the American colonies
options.B: The 10 per cent interest charged by lenders on state loans
options.C: The annual maintenance cost of the Bastille
options.D: The salaries of the 745 members of the National Assembly

questions.explanation: Lenders who gave the state credit began to charge 10 per cent interest on loans, forcing the government to use a huge budget share for interest[cite: 67, 68].

questions.question: In the 'Society of Estates', what percentage of land was owned by the Nobility, the Church, and richer members of the Third Estate combined?

options.A: 90 per cent
options.B: 75 per cent
options.C: 60 per cent
options.D: 10 per cent

questions.explanation: About 60 per cent of the land was owned by nobles, the Church, and other richer members of the third estate[cite: 76].

questions.question: Which of the following was NOT a 'natural and inalienable' right as established by the Constitution of 1791?

options.A: Right to life
options.B: Freedom of speech
options.C: Equality before law
options.D: Right to property for all citizens including women

questions.explanation: While property was a 'sacred and inviolable right' [cite: 359], the Constitution of 1791 reduced women to 'passive citizens' without political rights[cite: 312, 535].

questions.question: Analyze the role of the 'Peoples' Militia' formed on 14 July 1789. What was their primary immediate objective before marching to the Bastille?

options.A: To execute the King at Versailles
options.B: To break into government buildings in search of arms
options.C: To release the hundreds of prisoners in the Bastille
options.D: To protest against the high price of salt

questions.explanation: The militia broke into a number of government buildings in search of arms before marching to the eastern part of the city[cite: 39, 40].

questions.question: How did the revolutionary journalist Jean-Paul Marat critique the Constitution of 1791 in his newspaper 'L’Ami du peuple'?

options.A: He praised it for giving the poor a voice
options.B: He argued that the task of representing the people had been given only to the rich
options.C: He claimed that absolute monarchy was better than the new system
options.D: He advocated for the immediate abolition of the Directory

questions.explanation: Marat noted that the task of representing the people had been given to the rich and that wealth influences the law[cite: 338, 339].

questions.question: What was the specific outcome of the 'Great Fear' (Grande Peur) in the French countryside?

options.A: The King ordered the army to open fire on peasants
options.B: Peasants burnt down documents containing records of manorial dues
options.C: The National Assembly was forced to move to the countryside
options.D: The price of bread was immediately lowered by the lords

questions.explanation: Frenzied with fear, peasants attacked chateaux and burnt documents containing records of manorial dues[cite: 238, 239].

questions.question: Regarding the Estates General of 1789, how many total 'grievances' were brought by the representatives of the Third Estate?

options.C: 40,000 letters
options.D: 2 billion livres

questions.explanation: The representatives brought some 40,000 letters listing grievances and demands[cite: 197].

questions.question: In the Political System of 1791, who had the power to exercise a 'Veto' over the National Assembly?

options.A: The Judge
options.B: The Electors
options.C: The King
options.D: The Ministers

questions.explanation: According to Figure 7, the King held the power of 'Veto' over the National Assembly[cite: 294, 296].

questions.question: Which social groups formed the 'Society of Revolutionary and Republican Women'?

options.A: Daughters of the Nobility
options.B: Nuns from the former convents
options.C: Women of the Third Estate seeking equal political rights
options.D: Supporters of Marie Antoinette

questions.explanation: This was the most famous of sixty women's clubs that demanded the same political rights as men[cite: 523, 524, 534].

questions.question: What was the financial value of the assets acquired by the government after confiscating Church lands?

options.A: 1 billion livres
options.B: 2 billion livres
options.C: 3 million livres
options.D: 10 per cent of the national budget

questions.explanation: As a result of confiscating Church lands, the government acquired assets worth at least 2 billion livres[cite: 245].

questions.question: Which symbolic image was used by the artist Le Barbier to personify 'the Law' in the Declaration of Rights?

options.A: The broken chain
options.B: The winged woman
options.C: The Sceptre
options.D: The Red Phrygian cap

questions.explanation: The winged woman is the personification of the law[cite: 385, 386].

questions.question: Why did the 'Convention' of 1794 eventually fail to maintain the abolition of slavery in the long term?

options.A: The slaves refused to be 'civilized'
options.B: Napoleon Bonaparte reintroduced slavery ten years later
options.C: The National Assembly voted against the Convention
options.D: African chieftains stopped selling slaves to French merchants

questions.explanation: The abolition turned out to be a short-term measure as Napoleon reintroduced slavery ten years later[cite: 607].

questions.question: In Robespierre's 1794 speech, how did he define 'Terror' in the context of a democratic government?

options.A: An unfortunate necessity that should be moderated
options.B: Justice that is swift, severe, and inflexible
options.C: A tool used only against foreign invaders like Prussia
options.D: A system to redistribute wealth to the poor

questions.explanation: Robespierre stated that 'Terror is nothing but justice, swift, severe and inflexible'[cite: 488].

questions.question: Who was Georges Danton according to the 'lived experiences' in Source A?

options.A: A noble who fled to Austria
options.B: A man who felt the system provided education without a field to utilize his talents
options.C: The inventor of the fat-reducing press
options.D: A supporter of the absolute right of the monarch

questions.explanation: Danton wrote that the system provided education without offering a field where talents could be utilized[cite: 172].

questions.question: Which group was excluded from the 'Active Citizens' category despite being part of the Third Estate?

options.A: All women and men under 25
options.B: Only the servants and landless labour
options.C: Merchants and lawyers
options.D: Nobles who had lost their titles

questions.explanation: The remaining men (under 25 or not paying enough tax) and all women were classed as passive citizens[cite: 311, 312].

questions.question: What was the significance of the year 1804 in the timeline of the French Revolution?

options.A: The defeat of Napoleon at Waterloo
options.B: The execution of Robespierre
options.C: Napoleon Bonaparte crowned himself Emperor of France
options.D: The first meeting of the Estates General since 1614

questions.explanation: In 1804, Napoleon Bonaparte crowned himself Emperor of France[cite: 650].

questions.question: Olympe de Gouges criticized the Jacobin government in 1793 for which specific action?

options.A: Increasing the price of bread
options.B: Forcibly closing down women's clubs
options.C: The execution of Louis XVI
options.D: Granting the right to vote to all men

questions.explanation: In 1793, Olympe de Gouges criticized the Jacobin government for forcibly closing down women's clubs[cite: 556].

questions.question: Analyze the 'Directory' period. Why did it lead to the rise of a military dictator like Napoleon?

options.A: The Directors were too powerful and popular
options.B: The political instability caused by clashes between Directors and legislative councils
options.C: The King of Prussia successfully invaded Paris
options.D: The Jacobins regained power through a second revolution

questions.explanation: The political instability of the Directory paved the way for the rise of Napoleon Bonaparte[cite: 505].

questions.question: What did the 'Sceptre' represent in the political symbols of the time?

options.A: The unity of the Third Estate
options.B: The symbol of royal power
options.C: The end of ignorance
options.D: The personification of the law

questions.explanation: The Sceptre was the symbol of royal power[cite: 373].

questions.question: According to Figure 2, which specific group within the Third Estate was 'rich'?

options.A: Small peasants
options.B: Big businessmen and merchants
options.C: Landless labour
options.D: Daily-wage workers

questions.explanation: Within the Third Estate, big businessmen, merchants, court officials, and lawyers were the more prosperous groups[cite: 89, 93].

questions.question: What was the 'Law Tablet' symbol intended to communicate?

options.A: That laws apply only to the rich
options.B: That the law is the same for all and all are equal before it
options.C: The ten commandments of the Church
options.D: The specific taxes to be paid by the Third Estate

questions.explanation: The Law Tablet symbolizes that the law is the same for all and all are equal before it[cite: 392].

questions.question: Which French city gave its name to the patriotic song composed by Roget de L’Isle?

options.A: Paris
options.B: Versailles
options.C: Marseilles
options.D: Bordeaux

questions.explanation: The song got its name because it was sung for the first time by volunteers from Marseilles[cite: 412].

questions.question: What happened to the 'souvenirs' of the Bastille after its demolition?

options.A: They were thrown into the Seine river
options.B: Stone fragments were sold in the markets
options.C: They were used to build the new National Assembly
options.D: They were returned to the royal family

questions.explanation: The fortress was demolished and its stone fragments were sold in the markets to those who wished to keep a souvenir[cite: 44].

questions.question: The 'Snake biting its tail to form a ring' symbol represents:

options.A: The poisonous nature of monarchy
options.B: Eternity (neither beginning nor end)
options.C: The three estates joined together
options.D: The threat of foreign invasion

questions.explanation: The snake biting its tail is the symbol of Eternity[cite: 372].

questions.question: Which of the following was a 'new' social right introduced for women by the revolutionary government in its early years?

options.A: Right to vote in national elections
options.B: Right to hold high political office
options.C: Marriage made into a contract entered into freely and registered under civil law
options.D: Exemption from paying taxes like the taille

questions.explanation: Early laws made marriage a contract entered into freely and registered under civil law[cite: 541].
